topic: Absolute Value Inequalities and Their Applications

questions.question: What is the solution to the inequality $|x - 3| < 2$?
The distance between two planets is $|x + 5| + 2$ miles. If the distance is less than 11 miles, what is the range of $x$?
The absolute value of the force $F$ applied to an object is given by $|F - 10| > 5$. What are the possible values of $F$?
The inequality $|x| > 4$ can be written in interval notation as
A spaceship is traveling at a speed of $|v - 20|$ km/s. If the speed is greater than 10 km/s, what are the possible values of $v$?
The inequality $|x + 2| leq 3$ can be solved by

options.A: graphing the function $y = |x + 2|$ and finding the $x$-values where $y leq 3$
options.B: finding the $x$-values where $-3 leq x + 2 leq 3$
options.D: graphing the function $y = x + 2$ and finding the $x$-values where $y leq 3$

questions.question: The temperature $T$ of a planet is given by $|T - 20| < 5$. What are the possible values of $T$?
A lab experiment involves measuring the magnitude of a force $F$ with an error of $|F - 10| > 2$. What are the possible values of $F$?
The distance between two asteroids is given by $|d - 100| + 20$ miles. If the distance is less than 150 miles, find the range of $d$ and express your answer in interval notation.
The speed $v$ of a spacecraft is given by $|v - 50|$ km/s. If the speed is greater than 30 km/s, find the possible values of $v$ and graph the solution set on a number line.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Use a number line to visualize the solution sets of absolute value inequalities.
Tip 2: Remember that the absolute value of a quantity is always non-negative.
Tip 3: When solving absolute value inequalities, consider both the positive and negative cases.
